response.justification: While the candidate demonstrates 19 years of software development experience with strong engineering practices and full-stack capabilities, they are fundamentally misaligned with this role. Their C++ experience is minimal (6 months at National Instruments in 2015) and outdated; they've spent the last decade in web development (TypeScript, Node.js, React). The role requires deep expertise in C++, systems programming, multi-threading, and industrial automation/IoT/cybersecurity domains—none of which are evidenced in the resume. This is a domain mismatch, not a seniority one.
strengths: 19 years of professional software development with proven ability to deliver across multiple domains and languages
Strong commitment to software craftsmanship, TDD, CI/CD, and Agile methodologies demonstrated across multiple senior roles
Track record of mentoring, knowledge sharing, and building platforms (GISCollective, open-source libraries), showing communication and leadership capability
concerns: C++ experience is minimal (6 months in 2015) and non-existent in the last 10 years; primary expertise is in modern web/scripting languages (TypeScript, Node.js)
No evidence of industrial automation, IoT, embedded systems, or cybersecurity domain knowledge; no mention of SDL, IEC 62443, or relevant industrial protocols
No systems-level programming experience with networking, multi-threading, or low-latency constraints typical of industrial machinery platforms; recent focus on high-level application development

key_factors.factor: Strong engineering practices and mentorship track record
